summary: Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials applied to the vibrational physics of doped GeTe thermoelectric systems at a fraction of DFT computational cost.
title: Study of Low Lattice Thermal Conductivity in Doped GeTe using Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
description:
GeTe is a prominent thermoelectric material, with experimentally reported zT values reaching up to 2.6 upon doping. Understanding the microscopic origin of its low lattice thermal conductivity is essential for designing high-performance thermoelectric materials. However, the introduction of dopants substantially increases the computational cost of first-principles calculations, particularly for phonon and vibrational studies, where the reduction in symmetry is severe.

In this work, we employ Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials (UMLIPs), together with fine-tuning strategies, to investigate the chemistry and vibrational physics of doped GeTe at a fraction of the cost of DFT calculations. The project centres on scalable atomistic simulations of complex, disordered thermoelectric materials.
